title: Rectangle End Table with Shelf
summary: A clean-lined end table on 2x2 legs with a lower shelf that keeps the base from racking — a fast, forgiving first furniture build.
description:
An end table is the ideal starter piece: enough joinery to teach how a leg-and-apron base works, but small and cheap enough that a mistake costs a board. This one rides on 2x2 legs, wears a 1x12 top, and carries a lower 1x12 shelf tied in by a center support so the shelf can't sag and the base can't rack.

The build order is deliberate. Build the two leg pairs first, then join them with the side aprons and — importantly — the bottom support. That bottom stretcher does double duty: it holds up the middle of the shelf and it triangulates the base so the whole table stays rigid. Skip it and you get a wobbly table with a bowed shelf.

Everything joins with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" screws for the 1-by parts and 2-1/2" screws where 2x2 meets 2x2. The top can be a glued-up 1x12 or, as one builder did, a piece of 3/4" plywood — either works. Cut the top a couple inches longer than the shelf for a slight overhang, sand everything smooth, and finish to taste.

tools.note: Whole build. 3/4" holes for 1-by, 1-1/2" holes for 2x2 joints.
Optional for tacking the shelf while screws set.

cutList.note: Cut to the width of your 1x12 (expected 11-1/4").
Two side aprons plus the bottom center support.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 23-3/4", four end aprons at 11-1/4" and three side aprons and support at 37" from the 2x2. Cut one shelf at 40" and one top at 42" from the 1x12. Cut two top edge pieces at 42" from the 1x3. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to three 8 ft 2x2, one 8 ft 1x12 and one 8 ft 1x3. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the two leg pairs
steps.body: Build two leg assemblies, joining each pair of legs with the end aprons using pocket holes — 1-1/2" holes and 2-1/2" screws where 2x2 meets 2x2. Keep them matched and square.

steps.title: Join with side aprons and bottom support
steps.body: Connect the two leg pairs with the side aprons at the top and the bottom center support down low. That bottom support both holds up the middle of the shelf and stops the base racking, so don't leave it out.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Set the 1x12 shelf on the bottom supports and screw up through the supports into the underside of the shelf — easy and clean, with no visible fasteners on top.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Build the top from the 1x12 with the 1x3 edge pieces (or use a piece of 3/4" plywood), joining with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" screws. Attach it to the base the same way you did the shelf. Sand everything smooth and finish.

images.alt: Rectangular end table on 2x2 legs with a lower shelf
